channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 51 dead and more than 100 injured in nightclub fire in North Macedonia
item.description: SKOPJE, North Macedonia (AP) — A massive nightclub fire killed 51 people early Sunday in North Macedonia ’s eastern town of Kocani, and injured more than 100, interior minister Panche Toshkovski told a press conference. The blaze broke out around 2:35 a.m.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TESTORIDES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michigan judge's order for Walmart shoplifters to wash cars squashed by his boss
item.description: A Michigan judge sought to have Walmart shoplifters offer free car washes for their punishment, but the plan was overruled by a senior jurist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran denies aiding Yemen's Houthi rebels after US strikes and threat from Trump
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Iran on Sunday once again denied aiding Yemen's Houthi rebels after the United States launched a wave of airstrikes against them and President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would be held “fully accountable” for their actions.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: McLaren's Lando Norris wins wet and wild Australian Grand Prix. Hamilton finishes 10th
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren’s Lando Norris has won a chaotic rain-affected Australian Grand Prix, his first at Albert Park, with the Brit just managing to stay ahead of Red Bull’s Max Verstappen following a third safety car late in Sunday's season-opening Formula 1 race.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until the Iran-backed rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts welcome their newly arrived replacements to the space station
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Just over a day after blasting off, a SpaceX crew capsule arrived at the International Space Station on Sunday, delivering the replacements for NASA’s two stuck astronauts. The four newcomers — representing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tornadoes, wildfires and blinding dust sweep across US as massive storm leaves at least 32 dead
item.description: Violent tornadoes and high winds have decimated homes, wiped out schools and toppled semitractor-trailers as a monster storm that killed at least 32 people ripped its way across the central and southern U.S. Some of the initial storm deaths were reported Friday night in Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Roberson, Rebecca Reynolds And Julie Walker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Education Department staff cuts could limit options for families of kids with disabilities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For parents of kids with disabilities, advocating for their child can be complicated, time-consuming — and expensive. Changes at the Education Department are likely to make the process even more difficult, advocates for kids with disabilities say.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: St. Patrick's Day parade celebrates Boston heritage in America’s most Irish big city
item.description: It’s that time of year again when more than a million revelers fill the streets of America’s most Irish big city for the South Boston St. Patrick’s Day parade.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 32 dead in massive US storm after new fatalities reported in Kansas and Mississippi
item.description: PIEDMONT, Mo. (AP) — Violent tornadoes ripped through parts of the U.S., wiping out schools and toppling semitractor-trailers in several states, part of a monster storm that has killed at least 32 people as more severe weather was expected late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JEFF ROBERSON, REBECCA REYNOLDS and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade overnight aerial attacks after Putin sets out conditions for ceasefire
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine traded heavy aerial blows overnight, with both sides on Saturday reporting more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories. The attacks comes less than 24 hours after Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Key witness in American college student's disappearance has his passport seized
item.description: Joshua Riibe, who was seen with University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki before she went missing, no longer has his passport, according to his attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Here's where Trump's approval rating stands on key issues
item.description: President Trump's approval ratings are showing signs of weakening nearly two months into his second term. While his efforts to crack down on immigration seem largely well-received, Trump’s performance on the economy has taken a hit amid whipsawing tariff plans followed by stock market losses.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that parents need to know
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? Don’t let it spoil everyone’s vacation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Airlines gate agent punched in face at Dulles International Airport; passenger arrested
item.description: Police are saying that an airline passenger faces charges after he punched a United Airlines gate agent Thursday night at Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD).
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters: A “high risk” day of severe weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Heartwarming photos of Trump with Musk’s son 'Lil X' go viral
item.description: Heartwarming photographs of President Donald Trump walking to Marine Force One with Elon Musk’s son are going viral, with internet users doting over the joyful moment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Voters flood town halls with fears of Social Security cuts, putting heat on GOP over Musk and DOGE
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — One after another, callers on a telephone town hall with U.S. Rep. Bill Huizenga pressed the Michigan Republican about possible cuts to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: United Airlines gate agent punched in face, passenger arrested
item.description: Police said an airline passenger faces charges after he punched a United Airlines gate agent Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina sets date for 5th execution in under 7 months
item.description: A South Carolina inmate is set to become the fifth person to be put to death since the state resumed executions in September after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Airplane crashes in Greenville near airport
item.description: UPDATE: North Carolina State Highway Patrol Troopers said Memorial Drive is now open again n both directions after being closed down due to a plane crash Friday. GREENVILLE, N.C. (WNCT) -- A Beech A36 plane crashed in Greenville on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mekaela Muck, NewsNation

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration resumes immigrant family detentions
item.description: The Trump administration has resumed family detention of immigrants, a legal nonprofit said, reigniting a controversial practice largely curtailed under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4
